__fixture_tag__: VENDORED UNIVERSAL-FF PREDICTION — frozen NNP output, not measurement
target_property: low-temperature (-20 °C) capacity retention as a fraction of room-T capacity plus cycle-100 capacity retention for a vacancy-controlled Na₂FeFe(CN)₆ Prussian-blue Na-ion cathode

operating_conditions.chemistry: Na₂Fe[Fe(CN)₆] Prussian-blue analog, citrate-slow-precipitation, low-zeolitic-water content (<5 mol%)
operating_conditions.electrolyte: 1 M NaPF₆ in EC/DEC (3:7) + 2 wt% FEC

model_caveat: CHGNet predicts DFT-level formation energies + per-atom forces for the Prussian-blue Fe[Fe(CN)₆] framework + Na-occupancy phase space. The 80 % @ -20°C + 92 % cycle-100 values here are a PROXY-from-literature pinned with raw#10 C3 honesty: Faradion + CATL Na-ion commercial Prussian-blue cell baseline + Goodenough-lineage academic Na-PBA papers. SEMI-QUANTITATIVE — CHGNet frames the formation-energy convex hull; low-T kinetics + vacancy-driven cycle fade are downstream of the electronic-structure prediction. Note: cell engineering → hexa-energy per CROSS_LINK §3.3; this fixture pins material-layer capacity only.
proxy_source: Faradion 2024 technical brief (Na-ion Prussian-blue cathode commercial cell, low-T cap @ -20 °C ≈ 75-85 % of RT cap, cycle-100 retention ≈ 90-95 %); CATL 2023 Na-ion 1st-gen commercial cell launch (Prussian-blue + hard-carbon, low-T retention claim ≥ 80 % @ -20 °C); Goodenough et al. 2011 J. Am. Chem. Soc. 133:5388 (Prussian-blue analog Na-ion cathode early academic anchor); Lu et al. 2015 J. Mater. Chem. A 3:23357 (low-defect Na₂Fe[Fe(CN)₆] vacancy + citrate-control); HiNa Battery 2024 Na-ion product spec; Altris 2024 Prussian-white commercial Na-ion vendor
falsifier_text: cap @ -20°C < 80% RT cap OR self-discharge > 5%/mo → FAIL
delta_to_target_cycle100: PASS at 92 % cycle-100 retention (separate cycle metric)
unproven_markers_preserved: water-content sensitivity UNVERIFIED at production — zeolitic water leads to capacity fade
vacancy formation HARD_WALL during synthesis — Fe(CN)₆⁴⁻ release during citrate-controlled co-precipitation
commodity-scale citrate process UNVERIFIED at multi-tonne reactor
self-discharge ≤ 5 %/mo UNVERIFIED at Faradion / CATL cell-level (vendor claims, no peer-reviewed teardown)
cell engineering → hexa-energy per CROSS_LINK §3.3 — this fixture is material-layer only
honesty_caveats: Low-T cap retention 80 % @ -20 °C hits the F-BAT-CATH-3 floor exactly — MARGINAL on the low-T arm. Self-discharge ≤ 5 %/mo arm is the binding falsifier — Faradion + CATL vendor claims at 3-5 %/mo, no peer-reviewed teardown UNVERIFIED. PASS on cap-retention alone does NOT close the candidate; vacancy-water HARD_WALL + scale-up UNVERIFIED markers stay in place. Cell engineering belongs to hexa-energy per CROSS_LINK §3.3 — material-only anchor here. SPEC_FIRST preserved — does NOT promote to EXTERNAL-VERIFIED.
license_note: Vendor datasheets + peer-reviewed paper values per Faradion / CATL public technical briefs + CC-BY publishers; Goodenough group historical Prussian-blue chemistry (raw#10 C3, NOT n=6 lattice-fit).
